title: Mayor Walsh rejects most of common council's budget cuts, calls for compromise
summary: Mayor Walsh vetoed more than half of the Common Council's 81 budget amendments that cut $16M from his proposal, seeking to restore approximately $13M without a property tax increase. The dispute centers on protecting public safety and quality-of-life services, with a critical June 1 deadline for resolution before the July 1 fiscal year. Walsh cited lack of pre-amendment consultation and the council's failure to identify new revenue sources as limiting negotiation options.

political_signal.description: Mayor actively resisting council's budget-cutting direction; expressing constituent concern about service degradation and framing cuts as 'step backwards.' Signals potential governance conflict with two council members running for mayor's office unlikely to support override compromise.

processing_notes: No specific city departments identified by name in article; references are generic ('all departments').
Dollar amounts validated: $16M cuts, $13M restoration target, $27M (original reserve draw), $120M+ (total reserves), $14M (council's proposed reserve draw).
Critical deadline: June 1, 2025 for agreement; July 1, 2025 fiscal year start.
Override threshold: 6 of 9 council votes needed; currently 2 likely opposition (Hogan, Majok).
